message:
fix(security): add path traversal and SQL injection sanitization across 9 connectors

Systematic security hardening: all connectors that interpolate user input
into URL paths or SQL identifiers now validate those values before use.

URL path sanitization (rejects empty, slashes, backslashes, "..",
percent-encoded traversal sequences):
- Algolia: index_name, object_id in search/get/delete operations
- Amplitude: chart_id, dashboard_id, cohort_id path segments
- Mixpanel: project_id, report_id, funnel_id path segments
- PandaDoc: document_id, template_id path segments
- PostHog: project_id, insight_id, dashboard_id path segments
- Pulumi: organization, project, stack path segments
- Semantic Scholar: paper_id, author_id path segments
- WhatsApp: phone_number_id, message_id path segments

SQL identifier validation (rejects non-alphanumeric/underscore/dot chars,
validates leading char, rejects dot abuse):
- MySQL: table names in list_columns, list_indexes, query operations

Each connector also adds an InvalidInput error variant and corresponding
unit tests for the sanitization functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
